calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-03-2025 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-23-2025 Continued To Sep-03-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Appear (Re: Administrator Michael Y. Mak)
Off Calendar Jun-10-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-10-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-10-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Thomas LaLanne, Esq., for Michael Mak, Administrator.

At the outset, the court acknowledges the response to the OSC RE Sanctions and advises that it does not find good cause for counsel's failure to appear at the previous calling. Accordingly, the court orders counsel to pay sanctions in the amount of $250.

Counsel advises that a petition was recently filed with a hearing date of Jul-23-2025. The subject matters are taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Chwin. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-13-2025 Continued To Jun-10-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. An Order to Appear will be issued to Administrator Michael Mak and Attorney Thomas John LaLanne to appear on Jun-10-2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

An Order to Show Cause as to why attorney Thomas John LaLanne should not be sanctioned $250 for failure to appear, pursuant to CCP Sec. 128, shall also be set for Jun-10-2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Thompson. Not Reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Continued To May-13-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Thomas LaLanne, for and with Michael Mak, Administrator.

At the court's query, Mr. LaLanne reports as to his failure to appear at the last calling. After consideration of counsel's report, the Order to Appear is taken off calendar. Mr. LaLanne reports as to the status of the Estate and requests a continuance. After additional discussion, the matter is continued to May-13-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Loke. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Continued To Apr-01-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The court shall issue an Order to Appear to attorney Thomas LaLanne and Administrator Michael Mak to appear on April 1, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The status hearing is also continued to the same date.

An Order to Show Cause shall also be issued to attorney Thomas LaLanne as to why he should not be sanctioned $250 under CCP Section 128 for failure to appear at today's hearing. Hearing on Order to Show cause is also set for April 1, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-11-2025 Continued To Mar-11-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-24-2024 Off Calendar

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-24-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas LaLanne for and with Michael Mak, Petitioner/Administrator (via Zoom).

Discussion was held regarding Mr. LaLanne's declaration in response to the court's Order to Show Cause. The court found Mr. LaLanne's explanation unsatisfactory and imposed sanctions in the amount of $250 for his failure to appear at the 12/3/2024 hearing.

Mr. LaLanne reported that the commercial property on Bryant Street has been on the market for a significant period, but a cash offer of approximately $700,000 was accepted after negotiations. The offer includes a 48-hour contingency period, with escrow expected to close within 2-3 weeks.

The court took the status of administration hearing off calendar and set a new status hearing for the filing of a petition for final distribution for February 11, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-03-2024 Continued To Dec-24-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
None.

The court noted that neither the attorney of record, Mr. Thomas LaLanne, nor the Petitioner Michael Mak, appeared as required.

The court will issue to attorney Thomas LaLanne an order to appear on December 24, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to show cause why he should not be sanctioned $250 for failing to appear at this hearing.

The court will issue to Petitioner Michael Mak an order to appear on December 24, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to show cause why he should not be suspended as administrator for failing to file the required petition for final distribution.

The First Amended Sixth Report of Status of Administration is also continued to December 24, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas LaLanne, via ZOOM, for Michael Mak, Administrator.

The court begins by asking counsel for an update regarding listing and sale of the real property. Counsel reports that an agreement among the heirs has been reached to sell the property and it has been listed. But it appears as though the asking price may be too high and therefore, no offers have been received. The court then advises counsel that it appears as though the issue regarding proper notice has been resolved and reminds counsel that notice of probate hearings should be filed using the mandatory judicial council form, moving forward. The court advises counsel that it intends to grant the status petition but that it appears as though the court does not have a proposed order so an order will be issued by the court. Counsel reports that he has submitted a proposed order. The court advises counsel that it will search for the order or will issue its own order as previously stated.

The Accounting Status Petition is submitted. A Status on Close of Estate is set for November 16, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Mary Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: B. Tso. Not reported.
